metadata.title: Control of Nitrosamine Impurities in Human Drugs - Guidance for Industry (Revision 2)

rules.rule_text: All manufacturers and applicants of chemically synthesized APIs and drug products must use the three-step mitigation strategy: (1) conduct risk assessments, (2) perform confirmatory testing if a risk is identified, and (3) report changes to prevent or reduce nitrosamine impurities to FDA in accordance with applicable requirements.
rules.source_section: Section I. Introduction / Section IV.B
rules.verbatim_quote: this guidance advises API and drug product manufacturers and applicants that they should use the three-step mitigation strategy described in this guidance to (1) conduct risk assessments of their approved or marketed products and products with pending applications, (2) perform confirmatory testing if a risk is identified, and (3) report changes to prevent or reduce nitrosamine impurities to FDA in accordance with applicable requirements.
rules.exceptions: Risk assessment documents do not need to be submitted to the Agency but must be retained and available if requested.

rules.rule_text: The guidance recommendations apply to all chemically synthesized APIs, drug products containing chemically synthesized APIs or fragments (including biological products containing synthesized fragments), drug products at risk due to other factors, and semisynthetic and fermentation products that are at risk due to their structures similar to chemically synthesized APIs.
rules.source_section: Section I. Introduction
rules.verbatim_quote: the recommendations made in this guidance apply to the following: All chemically synthesized APIs; Drug products containing chemically synthesized APIs or fragments (including biological products containing synthesized fragments); Drug products at risk due to other factors described in this guidance (see section III.B., C., and D.); Semisynthetic and fermentation products that are at risk due to their structures, similar to chemically synthesized APIs
rules.exceptions: ICH M7(R2) does not apply to APIs and drug products intended to treat patients with advanced cancer indications as defined in ICH S9.

rules.rule_text: An acceptable intake (AI) limit for nitrosamine impurities must be determined as a level that approximates an increased cancer risk of one additional case in 100,000 subjects based on a conservative assumption of daily exposure to a mutagenic impurity over a lifetime (70 years), consistent with ICH M7(R2).
rules.source_section: Section IV.A. Acceptable Intake Limits
rules.verbatim_quote: An AI limit as defined in ICH M7(R2) is a level that approximates an increased cancer risk of one additional case in 100,000 subjects based on a conservative assumption of daily exposure to a mutagenic impurity in drug substances and drug products over a lifetime (70 years).
rules.exceptions: Manufacturers or applicants may provide a scientifically justified rationale to pursue an AI limit different from the FDA-recommended limit.

rules.rule_text: If the AI limit cannot be determined using the approaches described in guidance, FDA recommends that 26.5 ng/day be used as the AI limit (the threshold of toxicological concern for unstudied chemicals).
rules.source_section: Section IV.A. Acceptable Intake Limits
rules.verbatim_quote: If the AI limit cannot be determined using the approaches described in this section, FDA recommends that 26.5 ng/day be used as the AI limit.
rules.exceptions: If the total nitrosamine level is below 26.5 ng/day, no additional safety data beyond the data needed to establish that the nitrosamine level in a drug product is below 26.5 ng/day are needed.

rules.rule_text: When more than one nitrosamine is identified in a drug product, the total limit of nitrosamines should not exceed the recommended AI limit for the most potent nitrosamine in the drug product. An alternative flexible AI limit approach (Appendix C) may be used when multiple nitrosamine impurities are present.
rules.source_section: Section IV.A. Acceptable Intake Limits
rules.verbatim_quote: FDA recommends that when more than one nitrosamine is identified, the total limit of nitrosamines should not exceed the recommended AI limit for the most potent nitrosamine in the drug product. FDA also recognizes that if the recommended AI limits for the individual nitrosamines vary greatly, it might not be practical to base the total nitrosamine limit on the most potent individual nitrosamine limit and an alternative approach might be appropriate.
rules.exceptions: If more than three different nitrosamines are detected in one drug product, manufacturers and applicants are encouraged to contact the Agency.

rules.rule_text: AI limits are expressed in nanograms per day (ng/day) and converted to parts per million (ppm) specification limits using the formula: ppm = AI (ng/day) / MDD (mg), where MDD is the maximum daily dose reflected in the drug product labeling.
rules.source_section: Section IV.A. Acceptable Intake Limits
rules.verbatim_quote: Recommended AI limits reflect a permissible level of exposure to the impurity and are typically determined in units of nanograms per day (ng/day). For the purpose of establishing controls such as specification limits, conversion of an AI limit into parts per million will vary by product and is calculated based on a drug's MDD (parts per million = AI (ng/day)/MDD (mg)). MDD for purposes of calculating a control limit for nitrosamines is typically the MDD reflected in the drug product labeling.

rules.rule_text: Sensitive analytical methods with appropriate limits of quantitation (LOQs) are required to test whether drug products meet the AI limits. The detection and quantitation limit should be commensurate with the level at which the impurities must be controlled. The LOQ must be scientifically justified.
rules.source_section: Section IV.A. Acceptable Intake Limits
rules.verbatim_quote: Generally, sensitive analytical methods with appropriate limits of quantitation (LOQs) are needed to test whether drug products can meet the AI limits recommended for nitrosamine impurities. In accordance with the ICH guidance for industry Q2(R1) Validation of Analytical Procedures: Text and Methodology (November 2005), the detection and quantitation limit should be commensurate with the level at which the impurities must be controlled. The LOQ should be scientifically justified based on these principles.
rules.exceptions: Validation is needed to determine if a method developed and validated for detecting nitrosamine impurities in certain types of drug products is appropriate for detection of the same nitrosamine impurity in another drug product or formulation.

rules.rule_text: Step 1 of the three-step mitigation strategy: API and drug product manufacturers and applicants must assess the risk of nitrosamine impurities in APIs, marketed products, and products under approved and pending applications. Risk assessment documents must be retained and made available if requested by FDA.
rules.source_section: Section IV.B. Recommended Three-Step Mitigation Strategy
rules.verbatim_quote: Assess the risk of nitrosamine impurities in APIs, marketed products, and products under approved and pending applications. Risk assessments should be conducted in a timely manner based on the prioritization of drugs. Manufacturers and applicants do not need to submit risk assessment documents to the Agency, but they should retain these documents so that they are available if requested.

rules.rule_text: Step 2 of the three-step mitigation strategy: Manufacturers and applicants must perform confirmatory testing when there is any risk for the presence of nitrosamine impurities. Analytical test methods for nitrosamines must have specificity, excellent chromatographic separation, and highly sensitive detection capability.
rules.source_section: Section IV.B. Recommended Three-Step Mitigation Strategy
rules.verbatim_quote: Perform confirmatory testing when there is any risk for the presence of nitrosamine impurities. Due to nitrosamines' physiochemical properties (low molecular weights, some volatility, and high toxicity), the analytical test methods for nitrosamines need to have specificity, excellent chromatographic separation, and highly sensitive detection capability.
rules.exceptions: Testing using appropriately validated methods can be performed by the API manufacturer or by a qualified laboratory.

rules.rule_text: Step 3 of the three-step mitigation strategy: Manufacturers and applicants must report changes implemented to prevent or reduce nitrosamine impurities to FDA, including submission of DMF amendments per 21 CFR 314.420(c), changes to approved applications per 21 CFR 314.70, 314.97, and 601.12, and changes to pending applications per 21 CFR 314.60 and 314.96.
rules.source_section: Section IV.B. Recommended Three-Step Mitigation Strategy
rules.verbatim_quote: Report changes implemented to prevent or reduce nitrosamine impurities in APIs and drug products to FDA. This includes submission of any drug master file (DMF) amendments in accordance with 21 CFR 314.420(c) and changes to approved applications as required under 21 CFR 314.70, 21 CFR 314.97, and 21 CFR 601.12 and pending applications under 21 CFR 314.60 and 21 CFR 314.96.

rules.rule_text: Risk assessments must include NDSRIs (nitrosamine drug substance-related impurities) in addition to small-molecule nitrosamines. Manufacturers and applicants that previously conducted risk assessments without considering NDSRIs must reevaluate their risk assessments.
rules.source_section: Section IV.B. Recommended Three-Step Mitigation Strategy
rules.verbatim_quote: FDA recommends that manufacturers and applicants reevaluate their risk assessments if NDSRIs were not previously considered; in general, risk assessments should be revisited periodically as part of risk management.

rules.rule_text: All manufacturers of chemically synthesized APIs must review their API manufacturing processes and conduct risk assessments to identify the potential for nitrosamine impurities. If a risk is identified, confirmatory testing of batches must be performed using sensitive and appropriately validated methods.
rules.source_section: Section IV.C. Recommendations for API Manufacturers
rules.verbatim_quote: API manufacturers should review their API manufacturing processes and conduct risk assessments to identify the potential for nitrosamine impurities. If a risk of nitrosamine impurities is identified, confirmatory testing of batches should be performed using sensitive and appropriately validated methods.
rules.exceptions: If an appropriate risk assessment determines that there is no potential for nitrosamine impurities, or a risk is identified but no nitrosamine is detected, there is no need to take further action.

rules.rule_text: API manufacturers must optimize the design of the manufacturing process during route of synthesis (ROS) development to minimize or prevent nitrosamine formation, including: avoiding nitrosamine-forming reaction conditions; using non-amine bases when possible; avoiding amide solvents (DMF, DMA, NMP) when possible; replacing nitrites with other quenching agents; optimizing reaction conditions (pH, temperature, reaction time); and designing processes that facilitate purge of nitrosamine impurities in subsequent processing steps.
rules.source_section: Section IV.C.1. Mitigating the Presence of Nitrosamine Impurities in APIs
rules.verbatim_quote: API manufacturers should optimize the design of the manufacturing process for APIs during route of synthesis (ROS) development to minimize or prevent the formation of nitrosamine impurities... Avoiding reaction conditions that may produce nitrosamines whenever possible; when not possible, demonstrating that the process is adequately controlled and is capable of consistently reducing nitrosamine impurities within the recommended AI limits through appropriate and robust fate and purge studies

rules.rule_text: API manufacturers must remove quenching steps (e.g., using nitrous acid to decompose residual azide) from the main reaction mixture to reduce the risk of nitrosamine formation. The aqueous waste phase separated from the organic phase should be quenched with nitrous acid without contacting the API, its intermediate, or solvents intended for recovery.
rules.source_section: Section IV.C.1. Mitigating the Presence of Nitrosamine Impurities in APIs
rules.verbatim_quote: API manufacturers should remove quenching steps (when there is a risk of nitrosamine formation (e.g., using nitrous acid to decompose residual azide)) from the main reaction mixture to reduce the risk of nitrosamine formation... The aqueous waste phase separated from the organic phase should then be quenched with nitrous acid without contacting the API, its intermediate, or solvents intended for recovery.

rules.rule_text: API manufacturers must audit their supply chains and monitor them for any at-risk API raw materials and intermediates. They must maintain records including names of raw material or intermediate suppliers and manufacturers, and any repackers and distributors. When appropriate, additional specifications for at-risk materials must be established to prevent formation of nitrosamine impurities.
rules.source_section: Section IV.C.1. Mitigating the Presence of Nitrosamine Impurities in APIs
rules.verbatim_quote: API manufacturers should audit their supply chains and monitor them for any at-risk API raw materials and intermediates. API manufacturers should maintain records including the name(s) of the raw material or intermediate supplier(s) and the raw material or intermediate manufacturer(s) (if not the supplier), and any repackers and distributors who handle the materials before API manufacture. When appropriate, API manufacturers should establish controls and consider additional specifications for at-risk materials to prevent formation of nitrosamine impurities.

rules.rule_text: To avoid cross-contamination from recovered solvents, reagents, and catalysts, API manufacturers must use recovered material only in the same step or in an earlier step (if there is sufficient purification) of the same process from which it was collected. If recovery is outsourced to third-party contractors, the API manufacturer must audit the contractors' validation of cleaning procedures and other controls.
rules.source_section: Section IV.C.1. Mitigating the Presence of Nitrosamine Impurities in APIs
rules.verbatim_quote: To avoid cross-contamination when recovered materials such as solvents, reagents, and catalysts are used in the manufacturing process, API manufacturers should use recovered material only in the same step or in an earlier step (if there is sufficient purification) of the same process from which it was collected... If the recovery of materials is outsourced to third-party contractors, the API manufacturer should audit the contractors' validation of cleaning procedures and other controls.

rules.rule_text: API manufacturers must analyze nitrite and nitrosamine levels in potable water used in API manufacture and use water that has been purified to remove unacceptable impurities, since potable water may contain low levels of nitrites and nitrosamines from environmental sources.
rules.source_section: Section IV.C.1. Mitigating the Presence of Nitrosamine Impurities in APIs
rules.verbatim_quote: API manufacturers should be aware that potable water used in API manufacture may contain low levels of nitrites and even nitrosamines from environmental sources. The existence of nitrites in processing water may lead to nitrosamine formation in API manufacture. Therefore, to avoid unacceptable levels of nitrosamine impurities in APIs, API manufacturers should analyze nitrite and nitrosamine levels in water and use water that has been purified to remove unacceptable impurities.

rules.rule_text: If API manufacturers reprocess or rework API batches to control nitrosamine impurity levels, the quality unit must oversee any reprocess or rework of such batches, and reprocessing or reworking operations must follow recommendations in ICH Q7.
rules.source_section: Section IV.C.1. Mitigating the Presence of Nitrosamine Impurities in APIs
rules.verbatim_quote: If API manufacturers reprocess or rework API batches to control nitrosamine impurity levels, the quality unit should oversee any reprocess or rework of such batches. Reprocessing or reworking operations should follow recommendations found in ICH Q7.

rules.rule_text: If a nitrosamine impurity is detected above the LOQ, the API manufacturer must develop a control strategy to ensure that nitrosamine levels reliably remain at or below the recommended AI limit, accounting for batch-to-batch variations. For at-risk APIs with an impurity detected above 10 percent of the recommended AI limit, manufacturers must test each batch on release and the stability samples on the retest dates.
rules.source_section: Section IV.C.2. Control of Nitrosamine Impurities in APIs
rules.verbatim_quote: If a nitrosamine impurity is detected above the LOQ, the API manufacturer should develop a strategy to ensure that the nitrosamine level remains at or below the recommended AI limit. Manufacturers should develop an appropriate control strategy to ensure that the nitrosamine level reliably remains below the recommended AI limit in the API, taking into consideration batch-to-batch variations. Given existing uncertainties regarding nitrosamine impurities and their presence in drugs, for at-risk APIs with an impurity detected above 10 percent of the recommended AI limit, manufacturers should test each batch on release and the stability samples on the retest dates for the presence of nitrosamine impurities.
rules.exceptions: Alternate approaches (e.g., upstream test of an intermediate) should be supported by sufficient process understanding and evidence of adequate statistical control and should be submitted to FDA in a supplement prior to implementation, as appropriate.

rules.rule_text: Any API batch found to contain levels of nitrosamine impurities above the recommended AI limit must not be released by the API manufacturer for distribution.
rules.source_section: Section IV.C.2. Control of Nitrosamine Impurities in APIs
rules.verbatim_quote: Any API batch found to contain levels of nitrosamine impurities above the recommended AI limit should not be released by the API manufacturer for distribution.
rules.exceptions: FDA may exercise enforcement discretion when warranted to prevent or mitigate a shortage of a drug. API manufacturer should contact the Agency if the API may be needed to prevent a disruption in the drug supply.

rules.rule_text: Drug product manufacturers and applicants must conduct risk assessments to determine the potential for small-molecule nitrosamine impurities and NDSRIs in drug products, involving collaboration with the API manufacturer to identify the API ROS or other process conditions that put the drug product at risk. The risk assessment must also include evaluation of any pathway (e.g., degradation and nitrosamine precursor impurities) that may introduce the risk of nitrosamine formation during drug product manufacture or storage.
rules.source_section: Section IV.D.1. Risk Assessment and Confirmatory Testing
rules.verbatim_quote: Drug product manufacturers and applicants should conduct risk assessments to determine the potential for small-molecule nitrosamine impurities and NDSRIs in drug products. A risk assessment should involve collaboration with the API manufacturer to aid in the identification of the API ROS or other process conditions of the API's manufacture that put the drug product at risk for nitrosamine impurities. The risk assessment should also include evaluation of any pathway (e.g., degradation and nitrosamine precursor impurities such as dimethylamine or other secondary amine precursors) that may introduce the risk of nitrosamine formation during drug product manufacture or storage.
rules.exceptions: If an appropriate risk assessment determines that there is no potential for nitrosamine impurities, there is no need to take further action.

rules.rule_text: Drug product manufacturers and applicants must test representative samples of all incoming components, including lots of at-risk API, before use, as required under 21 CFR 211.84, and must continue testing each API lot for nitrosamine impurities until they have verified that the API supplier can consistently manufacture API that meets recommended AI limits throughout its expiry or retest date.
rules.source_section: Section IV.D.2.a. Establishing reliability of API suppliers
rules.verbatim_quote: Drug product manufacturers and applicants must test representative samples of all incoming components, including lots of at-risk API, before use, as required under 21 CFR 211.84... drug product manufacturers and applicants should continue testing each API lot for nitrosamine impurities until they have verified that the API supplier can consistently manufacture API that meets the recommended AI limits throughout its expiry or retest date.
rules.exceptions: Testing should continue if there are variations or an upward trend of nitrosamine impurities within the API expiry period that may lead to levels exceeding AI limits.

rules.rule_text: When identified nitrosamine impurities are included in the API specification and the manufacturer relies on the supplier's certificate of analysis for nitrosamine test results, the reliability of the supplier's analyses must be validated at appropriate intervals.
rules.source_section: Section IV.D.2.a. Establishing reliability of API suppliers
rules.verbatim_quote: Additionally, when identified nitrosamine impurities are included in the API specification and the manufacturer relies on the supplier's certificate of analysis for nitrosamine test results, the reliability of the supplier's analyses must be validated at appropriate intervals.

rules.rule_text: If a nitrosamine impurity is detected above the LOQ in a drug product, the manufacturer or applicant must develop a control strategy including specification limits for identified nitrosamine impurities in the drug product if confirmatory testing finds levels above 10 percent of the recommended AI limit, including in stability samples at expiry.
rules.source_section: Section IV.D.2.b. Establishing specifications in drug products
rules.verbatim_quote: If a nitrosamine impurity is detected above the LOQ, the manufacturer or applicant should develop a strategy to ensure that the nitrosamine level remains within the recommended AI limit. The control strategy should include specification limits for the identified nitrosamine impurities in the drug product if the confirmatory testing finds levels of the nitrosamine impurity above 10 percent of the recommended AI limit, including in stability samples at expiry.
rules.exceptions: A control strategy is also recommended when there is a significant risk of formation of nitrosamine due to the API structure, the API ROS, or the manufacturing process of the API.

rules.rule_text: Any drug product batch found to contain levels of a nitrosamine impurity above the FDA recommended AI limit must not be released by the drug product manufacturer for distribution and may warrant removal from the market, because such drug products may be considered adulterated under section 501 of the FD&C Act.
rules.source_section: Section IV.D.2.b. Establishing specifications in drug products
rules.verbatim_quote: Generally, any drug product batch found to contain levels of a nitrosamine impurity above the FDA recommended AI limit should not be released by the drug product manufacturer for distribution and may warrant removal from the market, because such drug products may be considered adulterated under section 501 of the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (FD&C Act), for example if they are not manufactured, processed, packed, or held in conformity with CGMP.
rules.exceptions: FDA may exercise enforcement discretion when warranted to prevent or mitigate a shortage of a drug.

rules.rule_text: NDA and ANDA applicants are required to submit field alert reports under 21 CFR 314.81(b)(1) if testing shows that any batch of distributed drug product fails to meet the specifications established in the relevant application, including specifications on nitrosamine impurities. BLA holders must report biological product deviations as described in 21 CFR 600.14.
rules.source_section: Section IV.D.2.b. Establishing specifications in drug products
rules.verbatim_quote: applicants holding new drug applications (NDAs) and abbreviated new drug applications (ANDAs) are required to submit field alert reports as described in 21 CFR 314.81(b)(1) if testing shows that any batch of distributed drug product fails to meet the specifications established in the relevant application, including specifications on nitrosamine impurities. Similarly, applicants that hold a biologics license application are required to report biological product deviations as described in 21 CFR 600.14.

rules.rule_text: When NDSRIs are detected at levels higher than the recommended AI limits, manufacturers and applicants must develop control strategies and/or design approaches to control NDSRIs within acceptable levels. Mitigation strategies include: screening excipients for nitrite impurities via supplier qualification programs; designing drug products incorporating antioxidants (e.g., ascorbic acid, alpha-tocopherol, propyl gallate); and modifying the microenvironment to neutral or basic pH.
rules.source_section: Section IV.D.3. Recommendations for Drug Product Manufacturers on NDSRI Formation
rules.verbatim_quote: Manufacturers and applicants should consider the following mitigation strategies when NDSRIs are detected: Screen excipients using a supplier qualification program that accounts for potential nitrite impurities across excipient suppliers and excipient lots to reduce the risk of NDSRI formation in drug products... Design drug products incorporating an antioxidant (such as ascorbic acid (vitamin C), ascorbate salt (such as sodium ascorbate), alpha-tocopherol, or propyl gallate)... Modify the microenvironment to neutral or basic pH in the drug product formulation.
rules.exceptions: Each manufacturer or applicant should determine the potential benefit and demonstrate the suitability of any formulation approach.

rules.rule_text: Confirmatory testing must be performed on at least three representative batches. If confirmatory testing indicates nitrosamine levels are not more than 10 percent of the recommended AI limit, a specification (method and acceptance criterion) is not needed provided the root cause is well understood and manufacturing process controls are established and validated.
rules.source_section: Section V.A. Assessment of Test Results
rules.verbatim_quote: Confirmatory testing should be performed on at least three representative batches. Based on the results, manufacturers and applicants should use the following factors to determine whether changes should be implemented to prevent or reduce the presence of nitrosamine impurities: 1. If the confirmatory testing indicates that nitrosamine levels are not more than 10 percent of the recommended AI limit, then a specification (method and acceptance criterion) is not needed provided the root cause is well understood and manufacturing process controls are established and validated.
rules.exceptions: Nitrosamine levels, and the need for controls, should be reevaluated during the process validation studies and throughout the product life cycle if changes are introduced in the manufacturing process, excipients, API, or other materials.

rules.rule_text: If confirmatory testing indicates nitrosamine levels exceed 10 percent of the recommended AI limit but are within the recommended AI limit, a control for nitrosamines must be established in the release and stability specifications. For approved drug products, this information must be submitted in a supplement as changes being effected in 30 days.
rules.source_section: Section V.A. Assessment of Test Results
rules.verbatim_quote: If the confirmatory testing indicates nitrosamine levels exceed 10 percent of the recommended AI limit but are within the recommended AI limit, a control for nitrosamines should be established in the release and stability specifications. For approved drug products, this information should be submitted in a supplement as changes being effected in 30 days.

rules.rule_text: If confirmatory testing indicates nitrosamine levels exceed the recommended AI limit and changes in formulation, manufacturing process, or packaging are warranted, manufacturers and applicants must implement changes ensuring nitrosamine levels remain within the recommended AI limit. For approved drug products, major changes must be submitted to the Agency in a prior approval supplement.
rules.source_section: Section V.A. Assessment of Test Results
rules.verbatim_quote: If the confirmatory testing indicates nitrosamine levels exceed the recommended AI limit and changes in formulation, manufacturing process, or packaging are warranted, manufacturers and applicants should implement such changes that are demonstrated to ensure that nitrosamine levels remain within the recommended AI limit. For approved drug products, when these changes meet the criteria described in § 314.70(b) or § 601.12(b) as major changes, they must be submitted to the Agency in a prior approval supplement.

rules.rule_text: If a manufacturer or applicant proposes an alternative AI limit for nitrosamine impurities or identifies an NDSRI not included on the nitrosamine guidance web page, a proposed AI limit or predicted carcinogenic potency category associated with an AI limit must be submitted to FDA for evaluation. For approved drug products, this information must be submitted in accordance with 21 CFR 314.70(b) or 601.12(b).
rules.source_section: Section V.A. Assessment of Test Results
rules.verbatim_quote: If a manufacturer or applicant proposes an alternative AI limit for nitrosamine impurities or identifies an NDSRI that is not included on the nitrosamine guidance web page, a proposed AI limit or predicted carcinogenic potency category associated with an AI limit should be submitted to FDA for evaluation. For approved drug products, this information must be submitted in accordance with § 314.70(b) or § 601.12(b).

rules.rule_text: For approved or marketed drug products, manufacturers and applicants were required to: conclude risk assessments by March 31, 2021; conclude confirmatory testing and submission of required changes for small molecule nitrosamines by October 1, 2023; complete a risk assessment for NDSRIs by November 1, 2023; and conclude NDSRI confirmatory testing and submission of required changes in drug applications by August 1, 2025.
rules.source_section: Section V.B.1. Approved or Marketed Drug Products
rules.verbatim_quote: For small molecule nitrosamine impurities, as previously communicated, FDA recommended that manufacturers and applicants conclude a risk assessment of approved or marketed products by March 31, 2021... FDA recommended that manufacturers and applicants conclude confirmatory testing of drug products and submission of required changes in drug applications for small molecule nitrosamines by October 1, 2023... FDA recommended that if NDSRIs were not considered in previous risk assessments, manufacturers and applicants complete a risk assessment for NDSRIs by November 1, 2023... FDA recommends conclusion of NDSRI confirmatory testing of drug products and submission of required changes in drug applications by August 1, 2025.
rules.exceptions: FDA may request an expedited risk assessment, confirmatory testing, or other actions based on available information that shows a drug product may have an increased risk.

rules.rule_text: Prospective applicants must conduct a scientifically based risk assessment for nitrosamine impurities in APIs and proposed drug products and perform confirmatory testing as appropriate before submission of an original application. Risk assessment and confirmatory testing information may be submitted in an amendment if not available at original submission but must be submitted as quickly as possible.
rules.source_section: Section V.B.2.a. Presubmission stage
rules.verbatim_quote: FDA recommends that applicants conduct a scientifically based risk assessment for nitrosamine impurities in APIs and proposed drug products and perform confirmatory testing as appropriate before submission of an original application. However, the risk assessment and submission of confirmatory testing, if appropriate, and changes to the DMF or application can be submitted in an amendment if they are not available at the time of the original submission filing. Such an amendment should be submitted as quickly as possible after the original submission filing to minimize any potential adverse effect on the application assessment timeline.

rules.rule_text: Applicants must report all changes implemented to prevent or reduce nitrosamine impurities in accordance with FDA regulations (21 CFR 314.60, 314.70, 314.96, 314.97, and 601.12). Holders of pending applications must update their applications through submission of an amendment according to 21 CFR 314.60 and 314.96.
rules.source_section: Section V.C. Reporting Changes to Mitigate Nitrosamine Impurities
rules.verbatim_quote: Applicants must report changes implemented to prevent or reduce nitrosamine impurities in accordance with FDA regulations (§§ 314.60, 314.70, 314.96, 314.97, and 601.12). Holders of pending applications must update their applications through submission of an amendment according to §§ 314.60 and 314.96.

rules.rule_text: If an API DMF holder makes process changes in the ROS as a result of risk assessment and confirmatory testing, the DMF holder must submit an amendment to the DMF and inform each drug product manufacturer or applicant that references the DMF per 21 CFR 314.420(c). If a batch of API is found to contain a nitrosamine and is reprocessed or reworked in any way, these operations must be reported in the DMF or application.
rules.source_section: Section V.C.1. Reporting Changes by API Manufacturers
rules.verbatim_quote: If an API DMF holder makes process changes in the ROS as a result of the risk assessment and confirmatory testing, the DMF holder must submit an amendment to the DMF and inform each drug product manufacturer or applicant that references the DMF (including those with pending and approved applications), in accordance with § 314.420(c)... If a batch of API is found to contain a nitrosamine and is reprocessed or reworked in any way, these operations should be reported in the DMF or application (as applicable).
rules.exceptions: If the original DMF is not updated within a reasonable time frame, the new or revised process should be submitted in a separate DMF.

rules.rule_text: Reformulation changes to an approved drug product to mitigate nitrosamines that constitute major changes (as described in 21 CFR 314.70(b) and 314.97) require submission and approval of a prior approval supplement before distribution. Reformulation of an approved drug product is generally considered a major change requiring a prior approval supplement.
rules.source_section: Section V.C.2. Reporting Changes for Drug Products Reformulated to Mitigate Nitrosamines
rules.verbatim_quote: As provided in §§ 314.70(b)(1) and 314.97(a), changes to an approved NDA or ANDA that have a substantial potential to have an adverse effect on the identity, strength, quality, purity, or potency of the drug product (i.e., a 'major change') require submission and approval of a supplement before distribution. Reformulation of an approved drug product is generally considered a major change requiring a prior approval supplement.

rules.rule_text: Stability data to support reformulated drug products must include manufacture and testing of samples from three batches. For approved drug products at time of supplement submission, applicants must provide stability data for 3 months at accelerated (time points 0, 1, 2, and 3 months) and long-term (0 and 3 months) stability conditions. FDA may also request 6 months of accelerated stability data before approval for application products.
rules.source_section: Section V.C.2.a. Stability data to support reformulated drug products
rules.verbatim_quote: FDA recommends that such changes be supported by manufacture and testing of samples from three batches of drug products. For approved drug products, at the time of supplement submission, applicants should provide stability data for 3 months at accelerated (time points 0, 1, 2, and 3 months) and long-term (0 and 3 months) stability conditions for these three batches. Applicants with pending applications should submit this information in an amendment. For application products, FDA may also request 6 months of accelerated stability data before approval if analysis shows the potential for an upward trend in NDSRI levels.
rules.exceptions: The expiry period of the reformulated drug product can be the same as the expiry period of the original drug product if the stability profile of the reformulated drug product is comparable to that of the previously approved drug product (except the nitrosamine impurities).

rules.rule_text: Drug products that contain or fail to adequately address the risk of nitrosamine impurities may not meet applicable requirements of the FD&C Act, including sections 501 and 505. Relevant records demonstrating compliance with CGMP must be made available for FDA review during inspections under section 704(a)(1) or when requested by FDA under section 704(a)(4) of the FD&C Act.
rules.source_section: Section V.A. Assessment of Test Results
rules.verbatim_quote: Drug products that contain or fail to adequately address the risk of nitrosamine impurities may not meet applicable requirements of the FD&C Act, including sections 501 and 505. For example, such products would violate the Act if they are not manufactured, processed, packed, or held in conformity with CGMP in accordance with section 501(a)(2)(B). Relevant records and other information that demonstrate compliance with CGMP requirements must be made available for FDA review during an inspection conducted under section 704(a)(1) of the FD&C Act or when requested by FDA in advance or in lieu of an inspection as described in section 704(a)(4) of the FD&C Act.

rules.rule_text: FDA's recommended AI limit for NDMA is 96 ng/day, derived from the TD50 value of 0.0959 mg/kg/day (rat), divided by 50,000 to achieve 1 in 100,000 cancer risk and multiplied by 50 kg assumed body weight. The AI limit formula is: AI (mg/day) = TD50 (mg/kg/day) x 50 kg / 50,000. When carcinogenicity study data are unavailable or of lesser quality, the Carcinogenic Potency Categorization Approach per the ICH M7(R2) addendum must be used.
rules.source_section: Appendix B: FDA Determination of Recommended Acceptable Intake Limits
rules.verbatim_quote: A summary of the AI limit derivation for N-nitrosodimethylamine (NDMA) is provided as an example... resulting in 0.0000959 mg/day NDMA, or approximately 96 ng/day NDMA... Hence, a daily lifelong intake of 96 ng/day NDMA corresponds to a theoretical cancer risk of 10^5 and therefore represents an AI limit when present as an impurity. When carcinogenicity study data for an impurity are unavailable or of lesser quality as described in ICH M7(R2) addendum, the predicted Carcinogenic Potency Categorization Approach is recommended by FDA for determining an AI limit (see the RAIL guidance).

rules.rule_text: When multiple nitrosamine impurities (up to three) are present in a drug product, a flexible approach for total nitrosamine limits must be used in which each individual nitrosamine is calculated as a percentage of its AI limit, and the sum of all nitrosamines must not exceed 100 percent (formula: sum of Xi/AIi * 100% <= 100%) to ensure total cancer risk does not exceed 1:100,000. The concentration limit in ppm is calculated as: AI (ng/day) / maximum daily dose (milligrams/day).
rules.source_section: Appendix C: Example of Control and Specification for the Recommended Acceptable Intake Limit for Multiple Nitrosamines in One Drug Product
rules.verbatim_quote: Each individual nitrosamine should be calculated as a percentage of its AI limit, such that the sum of all the nitrosamines does not exceed 100 percent. The calculation for total nitrosamines is described as below: Sum(i=2 to n) of (Xi/AIi * 100%) <= 100%. Where Xi is the amount of each single nitrosamine i in parts per million (ppm), AIi is the AI limit of each nitrosamine in ppm, and n is not more than 3.
rules.exceptions: If an approach is proposed for total recommended nitrosamine limits other than that described above, or there are more than three different nitrosamines detected in one drug product, manufacturers and applicants are encouraged to contact the Agency.
